description: Scaffold a new Go text file library package with tokenizer, parser, printer, and tests. Use when the user asks to "start a new <format> library" or "scaffold a new <format> parser". Skip when the user wants to add features to an existing text package (use `implement-go-text-file-library` instead) or when the target format is binary, e.g. `types.go`/`decoder.go`/`encoder.go` (use `new-go-binary-file-library` instead).

results.query: I'm starting a small Go library for parsing and formatting TOML files. Scaffold a new package called `toml` so I can start filling in the format details.
scaffold a new go text file library package for the ini format. call it ini.
kick off a new go package called `ldif` that I'll fill in later to parse LDAP LDIF files - tokenizer/parser/printer layout please
i need to write a yaml parser in go from scratch. bootstrap a new pkg called yaml with the basic boilerplate (tokenizer, parser, printer, tests)
spin up a fresh go pkg for parsing graphql schema documents. name it `graphql`. I'll fill in the real grammar from the spec afterward
create a new go package called `css` for a CSS parser - I want the standard tokenizer/parser/printer skeleton with placeholder tests that pass
start a new format library in Go for hcl (HashiCorp config language). I want to scaffold the package now and fill in the grammar over the next few days. call it hcl.
Could you scaffold a new go package called `sql` so I can start implementing a SQL dialect parser? Want the empty tokenizer.go/parser.go/printer.go and tests in place.
new go package for csv parsing pls. call it csv. just the boilerplate, i'll do the real parser logic
set up a new tokenizer/parser/printer package in go called `protobuftext` for the protobuf text format
in my existing toml package, please add support for inline tables to the parser. tokenizer already handles them.
scaffold a new go package called `png` for parsing PNG files - it's a binary format, types/decoder/encoder
create a new go web service called userapi with handlers for user CRUD endpoints
scaffold a new Python library for parsing our internal log format
in my existing yaml package, fix the parser bug where multi-line strings break when they contain colons
set up a new go package for a redis client wrapper called redisx with a Get/Set API
make a go CLI tool that converts toml files to json on stdout. just a single main.go is fine.
read my config.toml and print the database section as a Go map[string]string
explain the difference between a tokenizer and a lexer in compiler design
write a small go function that strips html tags from a string using regexp
